calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-28-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Nancy E. Lofdahl appearing by Zoom for Marcia Kirk (Adminstrator).

The Court stated that before today is a Petiton for Approval of First and Final Report on Waiver of Account; Petition for Statutory Fees for Personal Representative and that counsel is asking the Court to retroactively approve a contingency agreement. Ms. Lofdahl stated that she was representing her client and her client's sister, that it had been ongoing, and that she had to open the probate. The Court and counsel briefly confer. Ms. Lofdahl argued that they preferred to negotiate a settlement with Noreen's two daughters, apologized noting that it wasn't handled procedurally correctly, stated that she waived part of her fees, that she filed a Marvin action, and that she only filed it to make the claim. The Court addressed Ms. Lofdahl noting that under her logic no contingency would ever be approved, stated that there were a number of options to correct this error, that only after there is money that she made an attempt, and then inquired why the estate should carry these costs. Ms. Lofdahl stated that there was no guardian ad litem, that they were adults, and that she understands that it wasn't handled correctly. The Court noted that it was an end run by Ms. Lofdahl. Ms. Lofdahl stated that the decedent died intestate and that there was no will. The Court stated that the Probate Rules can be cumbersome and that the Court has a role to protect the estate. Ms. Lofdahl stated that she is not sure what she can do to remedy the issue other than waiving her fees which seems like a harsh result. The Court noted that it was inclined to base a reasonable fee, that there won't be a muiltiplier, and that what the Court is considering won't be what Ms. Lofdahl is asking. The Court took the Petiton for Approval of First and Final Report on Waiver of Account; Petition for Statutory Fees for Personal Representative matter under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Matter not reported.
